findings.line: His gaze shifted.
findings.reason: Generic gaze-only beat; it labels a turn in attention without character-specific physical work.

findings.line: Uther glanced at her.
findings.reason: Generic emotional beat from the stock verb list; it could belong to any speaker in the scene.
findings.suggestedFix: Uther turned to her.

findings.line: His jaw shifted once.
findings.reason: Generic facial micro-beat doing caption work instead of revealing anything specific about Arthas.

findings.line: Jaina's mouth tightened.
findings.reason: Generic emotional-label beat; the sentence stops at the stock reaction instead of giving a character-specific tell.
findings.suggestedFix: Jaina went still.

findings.line: His gaze shifted to Sylvanas.
findings.reason: Another interchangeable gaze beat in a scene already saturated with eye-direction shorthand.
findings.suggestedFix: He turned to Sylvanas.

findings.line: The words landed hard and flat.
findings.reason: Narrative over-translation; the line interprets Arthas's exchange after the dialogue already carried the force.

findings.line: The room tightened.
findings.reason: Abstract scene-state translation; it names the reaction instead of showing it through the people in the tent.

findings.line: The kind of man nobody remembered because three hundred camps had raised him in the same shape.
findings.reason: Essay-brain summary close: the paragraph inventories details, then caps them with a generalized thesis sentence about the aide's type.
findings.suggestedFix: He stood in plain campaign wool with dark hair damp at the temples and mud on the hem, built for camp anonymity.

findings.line: There was no room in the sentence for hope.
findings.reason: Paragraph-final emotional summary; the dialogue already left the pressure in the room without this interpretive closer.

findings.line: That was all. Not a full plan. The first hard edges of one.
findings.reason: Closing cluster that explains what the shouted snippets mean instead of letting the launch end on the shouted snippets themselves.

audits.dominantPattern: Pattern 4/5 — infrastructure and abstractions carrying human agency
audits.p5p6Sweep: Dedicated second sweep found fresh P5/P6 hits at lines 9, 255, and 365, plus a continuing cluster of route/city/scenery agency across the chapter opening and close.

findings.line: The road south stopped pretending before Hearthglen did.
findings.reason: Infrastructure is given self-aware deception. Real agent: the visible refugee traffic and damage make the route's condition obvious before the city itself comes into view.
findings.suggestedFix: South of Eastweald, the road showed the damage before Hearthglen came into view.

findings.line: The road ahead had no patience left for any of that.
findings.reason: The road is given an exhausted human emotional state. Real agent: the traffic pattern and worsening conditions have stripped away ordinary road order.
findings.suggestedFix: Ahead, the road had dropped all trace of ordinary farm traffic.

findings.line: When the line thins, the field feeds itself again.
findings.reason: The field is assigned self-sustaining intent instead of the concrete mechanic. Real agent: unattended corpses rise and create more casualties.
findings.suggestedFix: When the line thins, fresh bodies start rising again.

findings.line: The room from Scholomance tried to rise at the edge of memory and found no purchase.
findings.reason: A remembered room is given volition and failed effort. Real agent: the memory presses at Sylvanas and does not take hold.
findings.suggestedFix: Scholomance pressed at the edge of memory and stayed there.

findings.line: The kind of man nobody remembered because three hundred camps had raised him in the same shape.
findings.reason: Camps are treated as self-shaping institutions producing a man on their own. Real agent: repeated military postings, uniforms, and routine labor have made him visually anonymous.
findings.suggestedFix: He stood in plain campaign wool with dark hair damp at the temples and mud on the hem, built for camp anonymity.

findings.line: Ahead, the road to Stratholme ran between fields built to feed a city that might already be eating its own death.
findings.reason: The city is assigned bodily appetite and agency. Real agent: plague-tainted bread is still moving through the city's ordinary food system.
findings.suggestedFix: Ahead, the road to Stratholme ran between fields built to feed a city where plague-tainted bread might already be reaching every table.

audits.registerSummary: Sylvanas thinks in tactical, externalized assessments: spacing, discipline, motion, posture, leverage, threat. She can compress strategy sharply, but she does not drift into essayistic captions or abstract mood labels when the scene is actively moving.
audits.dominantPattern: A1/A5 — caption fragments and scene-state declarations

findings.line: There was no room in the sentence for hope.
findings.reason: Scene-state declaration from above the moment rather than Sylvanas's concrete read of Uther's delivery.

findings.line: Sharper attention.
findings.reason: Verdict-tag fragment pinned after Sylvanas's read of Arthas; it functions as narrator caption rather than active tactical thought.

findings.line: Sylvanas looked at the map, at Jaina's hand still braced over the quarter she had marked, at Uther standing behind the prince with fear locked down so hard it had become posture.
findings.reason: The final clause compresses Uther's visible state into polished thematic language that reads more authored than Sylvanas-in-moment.
findings.suggestedFix: Sylvanas looked at the map, at Jaina's hand still braced over the quarter she had marked, at Uther standing rigid behind the prince.

findings.line: Uther stayed where he was, watching the room become its own consequence.
findings.reason: The sentence sounds written about the scene rather than thought from Sylvanas's body; 'become its own consequence' is polished narrator phrasing.
findings.suggestedFix: Uther stayed where he was, watching the officers move.

findings.line: Something in Arthas's face moved. Not enough to name.
findings.reason: The second sentence is a caption fragment summarizing the expression instead of leaving the unreadable flicker unreadable.
findings.suggestedFix: Something in Arthas's face moved.

findings.line: The kind of man nobody remembered because three hundred camps had raised him in the same shape.
findings.reason: Generalizing construction that zooms out from the specific aide in front of Sylvanas to an essayistic type-statement.
findings.suggestedFix: He stood in plain campaign wool with dark hair damp at the temples and mud on the hem, built for camp anonymity.

findings.line: Not comforted. Answered.
findings.reason: Fragment pivot. Deletion test leaves the real clause ('Answered.') carrying the meaning on its own.
findings.suggestedFix: Answered.

findings.line: Jaina did not soften the answer to earn it.
findings.reason: Preamble negation. The sentence defines her delivery by what it was not instead of naming the actual delivery.
findings.suggestedFix: Jaina gave the answer flat.

findings.line: Jaina was not shielding what she'd learned there. She was already trying to ruin it.
findings.reason: Negated preamble before the real statement. Deleting the first sentence strengthens the meaning and keeps the scene moving.
findings.suggestedFix: Jaina was already trying to ruin it.

findings.line: Not fear. Use.
findings.reason: Fragment pivot. The negative half is scaffolding for the real label.
findings.suggestedFix: Use.

findings.line: Not a full plan. The first hard edges of one.
findings.reason: Fragment pivot. The sentence refuses the direct statement before landing on the direct statement.
findings.suggestedFix: The first hard edges of a plan.

findings.line: His gaze shifted.
findings.reason: Excess generic eye-direction beat beyond the chapter cap; weaker than the scene's more specific gaze beats.

findings.line: Uther glanced at her.
findings.reason: Another generic eye/gaze beat after the chapter has already spent its stronger, more character-specific eye beats.
findings.suggestedFix: Uther turned to her.

findings.line: Falric's gaze flicked from Jaina to Arthas.
findings.reason: Eye/gaze beat beyond cap; the line defaults to stare-tracking instead of a more specific readiness or calculation beat.
findings.suggestedFix: Falric checked Jaina, then Arthas.

findings.line: Arthas's eyes went to Sylvanas.
findings.reason: Generic eye-route beat; by this point the chapter has exhausted eyes as the default reaction channel.
findings.suggestedFix: Arthas turned to Sylvanas.

findings.line: Jaina's eyes met hers for one beat and dropped back to the table.
findings.reason: Generic connection beat routed through eyes after the chapter has already overused this channel.
findings.suggestedFix: Jaina met her for one beat, then bent over the table again.

findings.line: His gaze shifted to Sylvanas.
findings.reason: Repeats the same generic gaze-shift tic already used earlier in the tent scene; excess instance beyond the chapter cap.
findings.suggestedFix: He turned to Sylvanas.

findings.line: His jaw shifted once.
findings.reason: Facial-change 'shifted' beat beyond the chapter cap for vague expression-shift wording.

findings.line: Something in Arthas's face moved.
findings.reason: Vague face-change wording; another excess expression-shift beat where the prose names change without specifying it.

summary.verdictReason: 35 KILL verdicts across 5 guides (18 MODERATE+, 17 MILD). Dominant problems: eye/gaze repetition, generic dialogue beats, and repeated agency laundering onto roads/rooms/city abstractions.
